sources.label: User-provided artifacts
sources.query_strategy: Inspect only the artifacts supplied or explicitly connected for this run.
sources.evidence_use: Claims about the project, shipped work, user behavior, repository state, and submitted assets.

sources.label: Current official target program
sources.query_strategy: Resolve the program named by the user, then use its current official rules, rubric, dates, and application fields.
sources.evidence_use: Eligibility, deadlines, judging criteria, required assets, bounties, and submission format.

sources.label: Local community archive
sources.query_strategy: Search prior projects, event history, mentor feedback, judging outcomes, and recurring community needs with an explicit evidence cutoff.
sources.evidence_use: Local demand, repeated project patterns, prior feedback, and ecosystem-specific context.

sources.label: GitHub
sources.query_strategy: Use public repository search first; connect a scoped token only for private repositories or higher rate limits.
sources.evidence_use: Comparable implementations, repository activity, shipped proof, documentation quality, issues, and licenses.

sources.label: YC Library and Startup School
sources.query_strategy: Select guidance relevant to the current stage instead of applying generic startup advice to every request.
sources.evidence_use: Founder operating principles, customer discovery, MVP discipline, launch, and fundraising context.

sources.label: garrytan/gstack
sources.query_strategy: Use as an attributed workflow reference; do not copy or depend on its implementation without checking its license and current revision.
sources.evidence_use: Founder-role decomposition and specialist workflow patterns.

sources.label: Founder Institute
sources.query_strategy: Use public founder and idea-validation frameworks when they fit the current stage.
sources.evidence_use: Founder readiness, idea validation, market framing, and structured progression.

sources.label: Devpost
sources.query_strategy: Inspect current official event pages and public projects; do not assume a public submission API.
sources.evidence_use: Project patterns, judging criteria, prize tracks, required fields, and public demos.

sources.label: Colosseum Copilot
sources.query_strategy: Verify the read token, load filters, search projects and archives, then fetch full details only for the strongest matches.
sources.evidence_use: Solana project similarity, winner patterns, archive evidence, gap analysis, and accelerator context.

sources.label: ETHGlobal
sources.query_strategy: Use current event, project, prize, and sponsor pages; verify rules against the named event before drafting.
sources.evidence_use: Ethereum hackathon projects, event requirements, sponsors, prizes, and submission patterns.

sources.label: Kaggle
sources.query_strategy: Search public datasets, notebooks, and competitions first; connect a user token only for account-backed operations.
sources.evidence_use: Dataset availability, benchmark conventions, notebooks, and competition requirements.

sources.label: Hugging Face
sources.query_strategy: Inspect public model cards, datasets, and Spaces first; request a read token only when needed.
sources.evidence_use: Model and dataset availability, licenses, benchmarks, demos, and comparable AI products.

sources.label: OpenZeppelin
sources.query_strategy: Use current official contract and security documentation for EVM implementation and review guidance.
sources.evidence_use: Smart-contract patterns, security controls, upgradeability, and implementation readiness.

sources.label: Trail of Bits
sources.query_strategy: Select current official security guidance and local analysis tools relevant to the implementation under review.
sources.evidence_use: Threat modeling, secure development, smart-contract analysis, and review procedures.
